_description: Rules applied per question for Canada vs Morocco 2026-07-04

per_question.note: Anytime scorer mid=0.1981 (liquid). Shaded slightly down: all 3 group goals came in the Qatar blowout (2 red cards for Qatar), zero in 3 'normal' competitive games.

Score-or-assist offer=0.3401 (illiquid) x0.945=0.3214. Both his group involvements came in the same Haiti blowout; zero in the other 3 games. Kept close to market given he's still a clearly high-usage, high-touch player (fullback overlaps, set pieces) even in quiet games.

SOT OU0.5 offer=0.2899 (illiquid) x0.945=0.274. Davies did NOT PLAY AT ALL in 3 of 4 Canada games (appearances=0) and recorded 0 shots in his lone cameo vs South Africa. Discounted well below market given both his uncertain minutes and zero shot output when he has played.
Penalty shootout Yes mid=0.1662. Direct market use.

SOT OU0.5 mid=0.5445 (liquid, tight spread), but Díaz has only 1 SOT in 4 games despite starting every game -- discounted below the market's more generous read per the postmortem's lesson that a real personal-zero (or near-zero) signal should not be overridden by team-context optimism.
Exact-goals '1 goal' contract mid=0.2205, matches Poisson cross-check (0.219) almost exactly.
HT result Draw mid=0.4546. Elevated draw probability (RULE8 fires, 28.78% overall) is consistent with a high HT-draw price.
Cards OU3.5 Over mid=0.4253, matches Poisson fit (0.418) closely.

Anytime scorer mid=0.3103 (liquid). Blanked vs Netherlands (his toughest opponent), but Canada are a considerably weaker opponent (17.4% win prob) -- reverting toward his 3-goals-in-3-earlier-games form rather than anchoring to the NED blank.

Market-fit lambda (3.025, from thin CAN SOT OU2.5 line) implies P(4+)=0.358, but Canada's own 4-game empirical SOT floor is 4 (never below it, in 4/4 games including a loss and a tense R32 win) -- empirical lambda=7.0 implies P(4+)=0.918. Initial blend was 30% market/70% empirical -> 0.68. REVISED per STRATEGIC_MARGIN_PUSH_RESEARCH.md §8.2: this is a genuine Tier-A signal (4-for-4 consistent, not a thin single-team-history guess), which the research says warrants MINIMAL shrinkage toward the less-reliable market line, not the ~30% weight originally given to it. De-shrunk to a 20/80 market/empirical blend -> 0.80. This is NOT a confidence-based push past our true belief (the research explicitly argues against that per-question, since it is strictly negative-EV) -- it is a correction of under-trust in a high-reliability signal, which is the one lever the research found to be legitimate absent any leaderboard-cutoff standing information.
Most-corners Morocco mid=0.519. Direct market use.
Goal_1_time_bracket sum(1-10,11-20,40% of 21-30)=0.400. Lambda-scaling cross-check=0.460. Blended 0.43.

margin_research_summary.principle_applied: De-shrink Tier-A (DIRECT market or >=4-for-4 empirical) signals toward their honest estimate; do NOT push any estimate past its honest true-belief value, since per-question RBP is strictly proper and a confidence-driven push is guaranteed negative-EV absent being behind near a discrete leaderboard cutoff (no such standing information available for this match).
margin_research_summary.questions_reviewed_for_margin: All 15. Only Q10 qualified: it was the sole question where a highly reliable Tier-A empirical signal (Canada's 4/4-game SOT floor) was being under-weighted relative to a thin, less-representative market line. All DIRECT-tier questions (Q4,6,7,8,11,14,15) were already un-shrunk market reads with no adjustment needed. Q1/Q3/Q5/Q9 (player props) already reflect real personal-data adjustments rather than crowd-anchoring shrinkage, so no further change was warranted there either.
